scope: doctrine truth-alignment + `safety_residual` lane closure + narrowed ExIt semantics/consumer prep + exit_target bridge helpers
artifact: research/design/HYDRA_RECONCILIATION.md; hydra-train/src/data/mjai_loader.rs; hydra-train/src/data/sample.rs; hydra-train/src/training/losses.rs; hydra-train/src/training/exit.rs; hydra-train/src/training/rl.rs; hydra-train/tests/integration_pipeline.rs
summary: Completed the documentation/status pass and closed the replay-derived `safety_residual` lane in code. Tightened ExIt to the stricter surviving archive form: compatible discard-only state check, child-visit-count teacher object, subset mask, and visit / coverage / KL gating, with RL consumer support for explicit `exit_mask`. Added bridge helpers to close the AfbsTree-to-training-batch gap: `build_exit_from_afbs_tree` extracts child visits and produces gated exit_target/exit_mask, and `collate_exit_targets` handles mixed-sample batch collation with per-sample masking. The full search-to-RL-loss path is now exercised in integration tests. The remaining gap is the self-play loop that constructs RlBatch in mainline training; `delta_q_target` remains open.
what_we_did: removed stale wording that described `sample.rs` / `mjai_loader.rs` as baseline-only
patched `safety_residual` from clipped `public_score - exact_risk` semantics to signed replay-derived residual semantics: `exact_safety - public_score`
kept `safety_residual` replay-derived, discard-only, and narrow
added loader, batch, augmentation, and loss-path tests covering signed positive/negative residuals and mask-gated aux loss
tightened ExIt semantics to compatible discard-only child-visit targets with subset masking and visit / coverage / KL gates
added RL-side `exit_mask` support and integration coverage for masked ExIt loss
added `build_exit_from_afbs_tree` bridge: AfbsTree root -> child visits -> gated `make_exit_target_from_child_visits`
added `collate_exit_targets`: batch-level collation of per-sample `Option<(target, mask)>` into batch tensors with zero-masked rows
updated integration test to use bridge helpers instead of manual wiring
not_done: no self-play loop or mainline batch construction code that constructs `RlBatch` yet
no `delta_q_target` closure yet

scope: advanced-head activation discipline (rank 4): density gates, gradient conflict tracking, warmup protocol
summary: Implemented the archive's gate pack from answer_13_combined.md sections 3.3, 5, and 6. Added density gates (per-head rho_h for dense heads, spp_h for sparse search heads), gradient conflict tracking (shared-trunk cosine monitoring), head-only warmup protocol with configurable countdown, and a controller that manages Off->Warmup->Active state transitions for all six advanced heads. The controller produces gated HydraLossConfig that zeros out unapproved heads. All 36 unit tests pass including full lifecycle, extract_target_presence with Burn tensors, and edge cases.
what_we_did: created hydra-train/src/training/head_gates.rs with AdvancedHead enum, HeadKind, HeadState, TargetPresence, HeadCoverage, GradConflictTracker, HeadActivationConfig, HeadGateReport, HeadActivationController
implemented density gate: dense heads require rho >= 0.8, sparse search heads require spp >= 5.0 (archive defaults from answer_13_combined.md)
implemented gradient conflict gate: tracks per-head gradient cosine, blocks activation when negative fraction > 30%
implemented grad_cosine_from_flat for cosine similarity on flattened shared-trunk gradient vectors (replacing grad_norm_approx which is just a loss-magnitude proxy)
implemented warmup protocol: Off -> (density passes) -> Warmup -> (conflict passes) -> Active, with configurable warmup steps (default 10k)
implemented extract_target_presence to extract per-head sample counts from HydraTargets respecting per-sample masks
implemented approved_loss_config that zeros weights for Off heads while preserving Warmup/Active weights
implemented warmup_heads() for caller to know which heads need trunk detachment

added 36 unit tests covering coverage tracking, conflict tracking, grad_cosine math, controller state machine, gate reports, target extraction with Burn NdArray backend, and full lifecycle integration
registered head_gates module in training/mod.rs
not_done: orchestrator integration: wiring the controller into supervised_phase_train_step / rl_phase_train_step (requires self-play loop infrastructure)
trunk detachment for warmup heads: model-level change to detach backbone output for warmup heads (requires model forward modification)
feature-ablation gate (Gate 5): requires evaluation arena infrastructure

scope: runtime ponder/cache provenance hardening (rank 5): trust levels, generation tracking, namespace enforcement

PonderCache now enforces generation freshness and trust-level gating. InferenceServer runtime cache-hit path gated behind Authoritative trust (nothing currently qualifies). PonderManager unified to use PonderCache. All tests pass, clippy clean.

added CacheNamespace enum for logical cache partitioning

added PonderResult::learner_only_stub() constructor

added PonderCache::invalidate() and flush()
replaced PonderManager.cache with PonderCache

scope: ExIt validation harness: shadow runner, report, thresholds, pass/fail evaluation
summary: Implemented the ExIt validation matrix harness from Agent 22/9/16 doctrine. The harness runs the live ExIt producer in shadow mode through the existing self-play pipeline, collects per-step metrics (emission rate, hard-state rate, coverage, supported actions, root visits, top-1 agreement, KL divergence), and evaluates them against configurable thresholds for a pass/fail decision. Gate-level rejection attribution tracks incompatible state, too few discards, not hard state, and other rejections. ExitValidationThresholds defaults are derived from archive doctrine: emission >= 1%, mean coverage >= 0.70, mean supported actions >= 3, mean KL <= 0.05, top-1 agreement >= 95%, min 1000 samples. 11 unit tests pass, clippy clean, full test suite green.
